metadata.summary: IaC framework selection for serverless (AWS SAM vs Serverless Framework vs SST vs CDK), copy-paste templates for REST APIs, SQS processing, Step Functions, and Lambda handler patterns with Powertools.

content.title: Serverless IaC Frameworks and Templates
content.summary: Framework selection guide (SAM for AWS-native simplicity, Serverless Framework for multi-cloud plugins, SST for TypeScript full-stack DX, CDK for complex type-safe infra) plus production-ready templates for REST APIs, SQS event processing, Step Functions workflows, scheduled jobs, and Lambda handlers (Python Powertools and TypeScript).

why.p: Choosing the right IaC framework locks in the developer experience, CI/CD integration story, and multi-cloud portability for the project lifetime. Starting from production-ready templates rather than bare-minimum examples avoids common misconfigurations: missing DLQs, ARM/x86 default, no PITR on DynamoDB, missing ReportBatchItemFailures on SQS consumers.

list.item: Starting a new serverless project and selecting an IaC framework.
Scaffolding a Lambda REST API, SQS processor, Step Functions workflow, or scheduled job.
Generating production-ready Lambda handler code with structured logging, tracing, and idempotency.
Setting up IAM least-privilege policies for DynamoDB, S3, EventBridge, or Secrets Manager access.
Existing projects with established IaC — switching frameworks mid-project requires full resource migration.
Non-AWS serverless targets (Cloudflare Workers, Vercel) — use Wrangler or Vercel CLI instead of SAM/CDK.
Need multi-cloud? → Serverless Framework or Pulumi.
AWS-only, simple project? → AWS SAM (low learning curve, YAML, native transforms).
AWS-only, complex infra with type safety? → AWS CDK (TS/Python/Java, constructs, CDK Nag).
Full-stack TypeScript with live Lambda development? → SST (best DX for TS monorepos).
Need plugin ecosystem (offline, prune, esbuild)? → Serverless Framework.
Infrastructure team managing multiple providers? → Terraform or Pulumi.
AWS SAM: High simplicity, AWS-native, no multi-cloud, no type safety, good local dev (sam local invoke).
Serverless Framework: Medium simplicity, plugin ecosystem (200+), multi-cloud via plugins, no type safety, good local dev (serverless offline).
SST: Medium simplicity, AWS-only, TypeScript type safety, excellent live Lambda dev (sst dev).
AWS CDK: Low-Medium simplicity, AWS-only, full type safety (TS/Python/Java/Go), manual local dev setup.

section.name: sam-rest-api-template
section.p: AWS SAM — Basic REST API with Lambda and DynamoDB (production-ready):

example.title: SAM REST API template
example.code:
# template.yaml AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09' Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31 Description: Basic REST API with Lambda and DynamoDB
Globals: Function: Runtime: python3.11 Architectures: - arm64 Timeout: 30 MemorySize: 256 Tracing: Active Environment: Variables: TABLE_NAME: !Ref DataTable LOG_LEVEL: INFO
Resources: HttpApi: Type: AWS::Serverless::HttpApi Properties: StageName: prod CorsConfiguration: AllowOrigins: - "https://example.com" AllowMethods: - GET - POST - PUT - DELETE AllowHeaders: - Content-Type - Authorization
GetItemFunction: Type: AWS::Serverless::Function Properties: Handler: src/handlers/items.get_item Description: Get item by ID Policies: - DynamoDBReadPolicy: TableName: !Ref DataTable Events: GetItem: Type: HttpApi Properties: ApiId: !Ref HttpApi Path: /items/{id} Method: GET
CreateItemFunction: Type: AWS::Serverless::Function Properties: Handler: src/handlers/items.create_item Description: Create new item Policies: - DynamoDBWritePolicy: TableName: !Ref DataTable Events: CreateItem: Type: HttpApi Properties: ApiId: !Ref HttpApi Path: /items Method: POST
DataTable: Type: AWS::DynamoDB::Table Properties: TableName: !Sub ${AWS::StackName}-items BillingMode: PAY_PER_REQUEST AttributeDefinitions: - AttributeName: PK AttributeType: S - AttributeName: SK AttributeType: S KeySchema: - AttributeName: PK KeyType: HASH - AttributeName: SK KeyType: RANGE PointInTimeRecoverySpecification: PointInTimeRecoveryEnabled: true
Outputs: ApiUrl: Value: !Sub "https://${HttpApi}.execute-api.${AWS::Region}.amazonaws.com/prod" TableName: Value: !Ref DataTable

section.name: sam-sqs-template
section.p: AWS SAM — Event-Driven Processing with SQS and Dead Letter Queue:

example.title: SAM SQS processing template
example.code:
# template.yaml AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09' Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31 Description: Event-driven processing with SQS and DLQ
Globals: Function: Runtime: nodejs20.x Architectures: - arm64 Timeout: 30 MemorySize: 512
Resources: ProcessingQueue: Type: AWS::SQS::Queue Properties: QueueName: !Sub ${AWS::StackName}-processing VisibilityTimeout: 180 # 6x Lambda timeout RedrivePolicy: deadLetterTargetArn: !GetAtt DeadLetterQueue.Arn maxReceiveCount: 3
DeadLetterQueue: Type: AWS::SQS::Queue Properties: QueueName: !Sub ${AWS::StackName}-dlq MessageRetentionPeriod: 1209600 # 14 days
ProcessorFunction: Type: AWS::Serverless::Function Properties: Handler: src/processor.handler ReservedConcurrentExecutions: 10 Policies: - SQSPollerPolicy: QueueName: !GetAtt ProcessingQueue.QueueName Events: SQSEvent: Type: SQS Properties: Queue: !GetAtt ProcessingQueue.Arn BatchSize: 10 MaximumBatchingWindowInSeconds: 5 FunctionResponseTypes: - ReportBatchItemFailures
example.note: VisibilityTimeout must be at least 6x the Lambda timeout to prevent duplicate processing while a function is running.

section.name: sam-step-functions-template
section.p: AWS SAM — Step Functions Workflow with retry and error handling:

example.title: SAM Step Functions template
example.code:
# template.yaml AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09' Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31 Description: Step Functions workflow with Lambda
Resources: ValidateFunction: Type: AWS::Serverless::Function Properties: Handler: src/steps/validate.handler Runtime: python3.11 Timeout: 30
ProcessFunction: Type: AWS::Serverless::Function Properties: Handler: src/steps/process.handler Runtime: python3.11 Timeout: 300
NotifyFunction: Type: AWS::Serverless::Function Properties: Handler: src/steps/notify.handler Runtime: python3.11 Timeout: 30
ProcessingStateMachine: Type: AWS::Serverless::StateMachine Properties: DefinitionUri: statemachine/processing.asl.json DefinitionSubstitutions: ValidateFunctionArn: !GetAtt ValidateFunction.Arn ProcessFunctionArn: !GetAtt ProcessFunction.Arn NotifyFunctionArn: !GetAtt NotifyFunction.Arn Policies: - LambdaInvokePolicy: FunctionName: !Ref ValidateFunction - LambdaInvokePolicy: FunctionName: !Ref ProcessFunction - LambdaInvokePolicy: FunctionName: !Ref NotifyFunction

section.name: sam-scheduled-template
section.p: AWS SAM — Scheduled Job with EventBridge Scheduler:

example.title: SAM scheduled job template
example.code:
# template.yaml AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09' Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
Resources: ScheduledFunction: Type: AWS::Serverless::Function Properties: Handler: src/scheduled.handler Runtime: python3.11 Architectures: - arm64 Timeout: 900 # 15 minutes MemorySize: 512 Policies: - DynamoDBCrudPolicy: TableName: !Ref DataTable Events: HourlySchedule: Type: ScheduleV2 Properties: ScheduleExpression: rate(1 hour) ScheduleExpressionTimezone: UTC RetryPolicy: MaximumRetryAttempts: 2 MaximumEventAgeInSeconds: 3600

section.name: python-powertools-handler
section.p: Python Lambda handler with Powertools (structured logging, tracing, metrics, idempotency):

example.title: Python Powertools handler
DynamoDB least-privilege read/write policy
example.code:
# Read access Statement: - Effect: Allow Action: - dynamodb:GetItem - dynamodb:Query Resource: - !GetAtt DataTable.Arn - !Sub "${DataTable.Arn}/index/*"
# Write access - Effect: Allow Action: - dynamodb:PutItem - dynamodb:UpdateItem - dynamodb:DeleteItem Resource: - !GetAtt DataTable.Arn

example.title: Overly permissive IAM policy
example.code: # BAD: Wildcard resource and action Statement: - Effect: Allow Action: dynamodb:* Resource: "*"
example.note: Never use wildcard Resource or Action in Lambda IAM policies. Agents frequently generate this pattern — explicitly reject any diff containing * in Resource.

example.title: Project structure
example.code: project/ ├── src/ │ ├── functions/ │ │ ├── api/ # HTTP-triggered handlers │ │ ├── events/ # SQS/EventBridge/Kinesis handlers │ │ └── scheduled/ # EventBridge Scheduler handlers │ ├── lib/ # Shared business logic │ └── layers/ # Lambda layer code ├── tests/ │ ├── unit/ │ └── integration/ ├── statemachine/ # ASL JSON for Step Functions ├── template.yaml # AWS SAM ├── serverless.yml # Serverless Framework alternative └── sst.config.ts # SST alternative

section.name: cli-tools
section.p: Essential CLI tools for serverless development:

list.item: aws sam (SAM CLI): local invoke, build, deploy, sync for AWS-native serverless. Docs: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/serverless-application-model/
serverless (v4): multi-cloud framework with plugin ecosystem. Install: npm i -g serverless.
sst: TypeScript-first IaC with live Lambda dev. Install: npm i -g sst.
cdk: AWS CDK for TS/Python/Go/Java. Install: npm i -g aws-cdk.
wrangler: Cloudflare Workers deploy and tail. Install: npm i -g wrangler.
aws-lambda-power-tuning: Step-Functions-driven memory/cost optimizer. GitHub: alexcasalboni/aws-lambda-power-tuning.
cfn-lint: CFN/SAM template static analysis. Install: pip install cfn-lint.
cdk-nag: CDK rule pack flagging IAM and serverless misconfigurations. GitHub: cdklabs/cdk-nag.

section.name: agent-prompts
section.prompt:
Create a Lambda function for:
Purpose: [description] Trigger: [API Gateway/SQS/S3/EventBridge/etc.] Runtime: [Python/Node.js/Go/etc.] Input: [expected event structure] Output: [expected response]
Requirements: - Idempotency: [yes/no] - Retry behavior: [describe] - External dependencies: [APIs, databases] - Performance target: [X ms p95]
Please provide: 1. Complete handler code with best practices 2. Error handling strategy 3. Logging and tracing setup (Powertools) 4. Unit test examples 5. IAM policy (least privilege) 6. SAM/CDK template snippet

Configure API Gateway for:
Endpoints: - [METHOD /path - description] - [METHOD /path - description]
Requirements: - Authentication: [Cognito/Lambda authorizer/API key] - Rate limiting: [requests/second per client] - Caching: [yes/no, TTL] - CORS: [allowed origins] - Request validation: [yes/no]
Please provide: 1. API Gateway configuration (OpenAPI or SAM) 2. Lambda authorizer code (if needed) 3. Request/response mapping 4. Throttling and quota settings 5. Custom domain setup
Generate AWS SAM template for: - [X] Lambda functions with [runtime] - API Gateway with [routes] - DynamoDB table for [entities] - Include proper IAM, logging, tracing, ARM/Graviton, and DLQ

section.name: agent-gotchas
section.p: Common agent mistakes when generating IaC and handler code:

list.item: LLMs default to arn:aws:s3:::* and dynamodb:* — explicitly demand Resource: lists in the prompt and reject diffs that contain * in Resource.
Generated Layers: ARNs are often wrong (region/version drift). Always parameterize via SSM Public Parameters or fetch latest ADOT layer in CI.
Step Functions ASL generated by LLMs frequently misuses ResultPath/OutputPath and mismatches States.TaskFailed retriers — human review of any Catch + compensating logic is mandatory.
For Cloudflare Workers, agents often miss the 50 subrequest / 30s CPU limits and produce code that works locally but throws Worker exceeded CPU time in production.

checklist.check: Set Architectures: [arm64] globally in SAM Globals.Function or CDK lambdaConfig.
Enable Tracing: Active on all functions for X-Ray distributed tracing.
Enable PointInTimeRecovery on all DynamoDB tables.
Set DynamoDB BillingMode to PAY_PER_REQUEST unless workload is predictable enough for provisioned.
Configure DLQ on all SQS queues with maxReceiveCount=3 and MessageRetentionPeriod=1209600 (14 days).
Set VisibilityTimeout on SQS queue to at least 6x the Lambda timeout.
Use SAM policy templates (DynamoDBReadPolicy, DynamoDBWritePolicy, SQSPollerPolicy) over hand-written IAM where available.
Add ReportBatchItemFailures to SQS event source for partial batch success support.
Run cfn-lint and cdk-nag on templates before commit — catch IAM wildcards and misconfigurations automatically.
